reports.title: Iran's Nuclear Sequencing Gambit
reports.subtitle: Tehran offers to reopen the Strait of Hormuz now and discuss nuclear issues later. Washington says that is not a deal, it is a surrender.

reports.title: The Tariff Laundering Operation
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court killed Trump's tariffs in February. He is now rebuilding the same wall using legal authority the courts have traditionally not touched.

reports.title: The Allegiance Test
reports.subtitle: Trump attended Supreme Court oral arguments on birthright citizenship, becoming the first sitting president to do so. The justices, including his own appointees, told him he was wrong.

reports.title: OpenAI Rewrites Its Contract with the Public
reports.subtitle: Sam Altman published five principles for the AGI era. The document replaced a pledge to help competitors if they got close to AGI first. It was not replaced with anything comparable.

reports.title: Affordable Cars Are Leaving the American Market
reports.subtitle: Nissan, Toyota and Hyundai have told the Trump administration that without a USMCA renewal, entry-level models no longer make economic sense to sell in the US.

reports.title: The Enforcement Problem No One Wants to Name
reports.subtitle: The UK, Norway, and Manitoba all committed to restricting social media for under-16s this week. Australia, which did it first in December, has found that 70% of affected children still have access.

reports.title: OPEC's Loudest Quitter
reports.subtitle: The UAE just left OPEC after 59 years. Saudi Arabia now runs a cartel where the most capable member is gone and three others are watching to see if they should follow.

reports.title: The AGI Ownership Fight
reports.subtitle: The Musk v. Altman trial starts today. The legal question is whether OpenAI betrayed its non-profit mission. The real question is who gets to own the most consequential technology ever built.

reports.title: Russia's Africa Corps Just Lost Its First Battle
reports.subtitle: In a weekend of coordinated attacks across Mali, separatists and jihadists killed the defense minister, seized Kidal, and forced Russia's Africa Corps to retreat.

reports.title: The Security Theater Problem
reports.subtitle: A gunman walked through a Secret Service checkpoint at the White House Correspondents Dinner carrying a shotgun, a handgun, and three knives. A vest saved the agent. A review will save no one from the next one.

reports.title: Beijing's New Veto Power Over American AI
reports.subtitle: China just blocked Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Manus. The founders cannot leave the country. Beijing is showing it can retroactively unwind US tech acquisitions of Chinese-origin startups.

reports.title: The Memorial That Normalizes a New Alliance
reports.subtitle: Kim Jong Un opened a memorial for North Korean soldiers killed in Ukraine. Russia's defense minister was there. Both sides are advertising a military relationship they previously denied existed.
